task_completion.description: Import the foothills_creek_survey.csv file into TopoCal.
task_completion.visual_evidence: TopoCal interface showing the successful import of points, with the filename 'foothills_creek_survey.csv' visible in an import dialog or the points rendered in the main workspace.

task_completion.description: Create a new layer named 'ZONA_INUNDABLE' with the color Red.
task_completion.visual_evidence: TopoCal layer manager or properties panel showing a layer named exactly 'ZONA_INUNDABLE' with a red color swatch or indicator next to it.

task_completion.description: Identify/select points with an elevation (Z coordinate) strictly less than 1845.000 meters.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A filter, search, or selection dialog in TopoCal showing a condition for Z < 1845.000, or a specific subset of points highlighted/selected in the workspace based on this elevation criteria.

task_completion.description: Assign the identified points to the 'ZONA_INUNDABLE' layer.
task_completion.visual_evidence: TopoCal properties panel, layer assignment dialog, or context menu showing the selected points being moved to or associated with the 'ZONA_INUNDABLE' layer.

task_completion.description: Export the points on the 'ZONA_INUNDABLE' layer to 'flood_points.csv'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: TopoCal export dialog showing the file being saved as 'flood_points.csv' in 'C:\Users\Docker\Documents\', or the Windows File Explorer showing the newly created file in that directory.
TopoCal 'Save As' dialog showing the project being saved as 'flood_project.tcl' in 'C:\Users\Docker\Documents\', or the TopoCal window title bar updating to 'flood_project.tcl'.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the TopoCal graphical user interface to perform the tasks.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent clicking through TopoCal menus, layer managers, and dialog boxes rather than using command-line tools.

integrity.description: The agent did not use terminal commands to create dummy output files.
integrity.visual_evidence: No PowerShell or Command Prompt windows showing commands like 'echo' or 'New-Item' being used to artificially create 'flood_points.csv' or 'flood_project.tcl'.

integrity.description: The agent did not use a text editor to manually type out the expected results.
integrity.visual_evidence: No Notepad or other text editor windows are used to manually write CSV data or spoof the project file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The threshold elevation is strictly less than 1845.000 meters. The target layer must be named 'ZONA_INUNDABLE' and colored Red. The final outputs must be exported to 'C:\Users\Docker\Documents\flood_points.csv' and the project saved to 'C:\Users\Docker\Documents\flood_project.tcl'. Note: Programmatic verification scripts are missing from the environment, so visual confirmation of the TopoCal GUI steps, layer creation, point assignment, and file generation in the File Explorer is essential for scoring.
